title: Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The internal temperature sensor has an open circuit, preventing temperature monitoring.
fullDescription: Error E-1 indicates the temperature sensor (thermistor) used for cooking and safety monitoring has disconnected or failed open. The microwave cannot regulate temperature for sensor-based cooking modes.
symptoms: Sensor cooking modes do not work

fixSteps.title: Reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes, then plug it back in. This can clear transient sensor errors.

fixSteps.title: Check if basic modes work
fixSteps.instruction: Try running the microwave on a manual time/power mode (not sensor cook). If it works, the issue is the sensor only.

fixSteps.title: Clean the interior
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the microwave interior thoroughly. Steam and grease buildup can affect temperature sensors mounted inside the cavity.

fixSteps.title: Call for professional service
fixSteps.instruction: Microwave internal repairs involve high-voltage components. A technician should inspect and replace the sensor.

whenToCallPro: For any internal microwave repair involving sensor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E1 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I still use my microwave with E-1?
faq.a: Manual time and power modes may still work. Sensor-based auto-cook modes will not function.

faq.q: Is it safe to repair a microwave myself?
faq.a: Microwaves contain high-voltage capacitors that can be lethal. Always unplug and discharge capacitors before any internal work.

faq.q: What does the temperature sensor do?
faq.a: It measures the air temperature inside the cavity to automatically adjust cooking time for sensor-based programs.

title: Temperature Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The temperature sensor has a short circuit, giving false high readings.
fullDescription: Error E-2 means the thermistor is showing very low resistance (short circuit), which the control board interprets as an extremely high temperature. The microwave stops for safety.

fixSteps.title: Let the microwave dry
fixSteps.instruction: If the interior is very steamy, leave the door open for 30 minutes to let moisture evaporate.

fixSteps.title: Clean and dry the interior
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe down all interior surfaces, paying attention to any sensor openings in the cavity walls or ceiling.

fixSteps.title: Reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. The error may clear once moisture evaporates.

fixSteps.title: Contact service if persistent
fixSteps.instruction: If E-2 persists after drying, the sensor needs replacement by a qualified technician.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor is physically damaged and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E2 sensor short circuit fix

faq.q: Can steam cause E-2?
faq.a: Yes, cooking very steamy foods can temporarily cause moisture-related sensor shorts.

faq.q: Should I cover food when microwaving?
faq.a: Yes, covering food reduces steam and splatter, protecting the sensor and keeping the microwave clean.

faq.q: How do I dry the sensor?
faq.a: Leave the door open and let air circulate. Do not use external heat sources.

title: Humidity Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The humidity sensor used for auto-cook programs is not functioning.
fullDescription: Error E-3 indicates the humidity sensor, which detects steam levels to automatically determine cooking completion, has malfunctioned.

causes.cause: Humidity sensor covered in grease or food splatter

symptoms: Auto-cook programs do not work properly
Food overcooked or undercooked on auto settings

fixSteps.title: Clean the interior thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: The humidity sensor is typically behind a small grille in the cavity wall. Clean the entire interior, especially around any vent openings, to remove grease and food residue.

fixSteps.title: Steam clean
fixSteps.instruction: Place a microwave-safe bowl of water with a tablespoon of vinegar inside. Run for 3 minutes on high. The steam helps loosen grease on the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes to reset the sensor circuitry.

fixSteps.title: Test auto-cook
fixSteps.instruction: Try an auto-cook program. If the sensor still malfunctions, professional service is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor element has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E3 humidity sensor fix cleaning

faq.q: What is a humidity sensor?
faq.a: It detects the amount of steam released during cooking to automatically determine when food is done.

faq.q: Can I use auto-cook without the humidity sensor?
faq.a: No, auto-cook programs rely on the humidity sensor. You can still use manual time and power modes.

faq.q: Does cleaning really fix E-3?
faq.a: Often yes. Grease buildup is the most common cause of humidity sensor issues.

title: Magnetron Overheating Protection
shortDescription: The magnetron has overheated and the thermal cutoff has been triggered.
fullDescription: Error E-4 means the magnetron (the component that generates microwaves) has exceeded its safe operating temperature. The microwave shuts down to prevent magnetron damage.
symptoms: Microwave stopped during a long cook
Microwave worked fine then suddenly stopped

fixSteps.title: Let the microwave cool
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave and let it cool for at least 30 minutes before attempting to use it again.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the microwave ventilation openings (usually on the sides or back) are not blocked by walls, cabinets, or objects.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: When running, you should hear the cooling fan. If silent, the fan motor may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Reduce cooking duration
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid running the microwave at full power for extended periods (over 20 minutes continuously).

whenToCallPro: If the cooling fan or thermal fuse needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E4 magnetron overheating fix

faq.q: How long can I run a microwave continuously?
faq.a: Most microwaves can run for 15-20 minutes continuously. Allow cooling breaks between long sessions.

faq.q: Is E-4 dangerous?
faq.a: No, E-4 is a safety feature that protects the magnetron from overheating damage.

faq.q: Where should I place my microwave?
faq.a: Ensure at least 2 inches of clearance on all sides for proper ventilation.

title: Door Switch Fault
shortDescription: One or more door interlock switches are not engaging properly.
fullDescription: Error E-5 indicates the microwave door interlock switches are not reading correctly. The microwave has three safety switches that must all confirm the door is closed before operation.
symptoms: Door appears closed but error shows

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door fully and close it firmly until you hear the latch click.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door seal and latch area for food debris, warping, or damage that prevents full closure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch hooks
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the latch hooks on the door. If they are broken, chipped, or worn, the door will not engage the switches.

fixSteps.title: Professional switch replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Door interlock switches are critical safety components. They must be replaced by a qualified technician.

whenToCallPro: If interlock switches need replacement. Never bypass door switches.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E5 door switch error fix

faq.q: Is it safe to bypass the door switch?
faq.a: Absolutely not. Door interlock switches prevent microwave radiation exposure. Never bypass them.

faq.q: Why does my door not latch?
faq.a: The plastic latch hooks wear over time. They can be replaced as a relatively simple repair.

faq.q: How many door switches does a microwave have?
faq.a: Most microwaves have 3 interlock switches that all must engage for the microwave to operate.

title: Turntable Motor Fault
shortDescription: The turntable motor is not rotating or is stalled.
fullDescription: Error E-6 means the turntable motor is not turning. The turntable rotates food for even cooking and some models require it to function.

fixSteps.title: Check turntable alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the turntable plate and the rotating ring. Clean the cavity floor and reinstall both, ensuring the plate sits properly on the coupler.

fixSteps.title: Reduce load weight
fixSteps.instruction: If the item being heated is too heavy, the motor cannot rotate it. Try a lighter dish.

fixSteps.title: Clean the roller ring track
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the circular track where the roller ring sits. Food debris can jam the ring.

fixSteps.title: Test the turntable motor
fixSteps.instruction: With the microwave unplugged, check if the motor shaft turns by hand. If the motor is seized, it needs replacement.

videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E6 turntable not spinning fix

faq.q: Can I use the microwave without the turntable?
faq.a: Some models allow it, but food will cook unevenly. Check your model's manual.

faq.q: Why does my turntable make noise?
faq.a: A dirty roller ring track or misaligned plate causes grinding or scraping noises.

faq.q: How do I replace the turntable motor?
faq.a: The motor is typically accessible from the bottom of the microwave. Unplug first and replace the motor unit.

title: Control Panel or Keypad Fault
shortDescription: The touchpad or control panel buttons are not responding or reading incorrectly.
fullDescription: Error E-7 indicates a fault in the control panel membrane or touchpad. Buttons may not respond or may trigger incorrectly.

causes.cause: Control board key matrix circuit fault
Sticky residue from cleaning products on keypad

symptoms: Wrong function activates when pressing buttons

fixSteps.title: Clean the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the touchpad with a damp (not wet) cloth. Avoid spraying cleaner directly on the panel.

fixSteps.title: Let the panel dry
fixSteps.instruction: If liquid got behind the membrane, leave the microwave door open and let the panel area dry for several hours.

fixSteps.title: Reset the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. This can clear stuck key errors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the membrane panel
fixSteps.instruction: If the membrane has failed, it can be replaced. This usually involves removing the outer case.

whenToCallPro: If the control board itself is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave E7 control panel touchpad fix

faq.q: Why are my microwave buttons not working?
faq.a: Moisture or wear in the membrane keypad is the most common cause.

faq.q: Can I spray cleaner on the touchpad?
faq.a: No, spray cleaner on a cloth and then wipe. Direct spraying can force liquid behind the membrane.

faq.q: Is the membrane expensive?
faq.a: Replacement membranes typically cost $15-$40 depending on the model.

title: Stuck Key Error
shortDescription: A button on the control panel is continuously pressed or shorted.
fullDescription: The SE error indicates the control board is detecting a continuously pressed button, either from a physically stuck key or a short circuit in the membrane keypad.

causes.cause: Button physically stuck from food residue
Moisture under keypad causing false contact

fixSteps.title: Unplug immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the microwave from power to stop the continuous beeping.

fixSteps.title: Clean around all buttons
fixSteps.instruction: Use a damp cloth to clean around every button on the control panel. Remove any sticky residue.

fixSteps.title: Press each button
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, press each button individually several times to free any that may be stuck.

fixSteps.title: Replace the membrane if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the stuck key error persists after cleaning, the membrane keypad may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the control board has a hardware fault.
videoSearchQuery: Galanz microwave SE stuck key error fix

faq.q: Why does my microwave beep non-stop?
faq.a: A stuck button on the control panel is sending continuous input. Unplug and clean the keypad.

faq.q: Can food residue cause SE?
faq.a: Yes, sticky food or liquid residue is the most common cause of stuck key errors.

faq.q: How do I prevent SE errors?
faq.a: Keep the control panel clean and dry. Wipe it regularly and avoid spraying directly on it.
